ORDER RE: TALESUN SOLARA USA LTD.'S MOTION TO QUASH MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC.'S DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO REED SMITH LLP (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD'S MOTION TO QUASH MARTIFER SILVERADO FUND I, LLCS DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO REED SMITH, LLP. PLAINTIFF SERVED A SUBPOENA SEEKING LITIGATION-HOLD CORRESPONDENCE AND RELATED DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANT'S FORMER ATTORNEYS. DEFENDANT MOVED TO QUASH, CITING ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. PLAINTIFF OPPOSED THE MOTION, ARGUING THAT DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE RELEVANT EMAILS. PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT THE EMAILS WERE IN EXISTENCE WHEN DEFENDANT BECAME AWARE OF THE POSSIBILITY OF LITIGATION AND SHOULD HAVE BEEN PRESERVED. PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT FEDERAL DECISIONS (WHICH CALIFORNIA OFTEN FOLLOWS) PERMIT DISCOVERY OF LITIGATION HOLD CORRESPONDENCE IN CASES OF CLAIMED SPOLIATION OF EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY ADOPTING THE FEDERAL DOCTRINE OF PRIVILEGE. FURTHER, WHILE CALIFORNIA EXCEPTS FROM ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE COMMUNICATIONS TO COMMIT OR PLAN A CRIME OR FRAUD (EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 956), THIS DISCOVERY SEEKS THE OPPOSITE: CORRESPONDENCE TO PREVENT A CRIME OR FRAUD. GIVEN THE ABSENCE OF ANY CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY SUPPORTING A WAIVER OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE HERE, THE MOTION TO QUASH WILL BE GRANTED. BECAUSE THE CASE PRESENTS A GOOD FAITH DISPUTE OVER A NOVEL ISSUE OF LAW, SANCTIONS WILL BE DENIED. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH DEFENDANT'S VERIFIED RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1-9 AND 21. IN SUMMARY, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S MOTION MUST BE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH FACTUAL GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND HAS FURTHER FAILED TO ESTABLISH THE REQUISITES FOR ANY OTHER RELIEF. NEITHER PARTY REQUESTED SANCTIONS AND NONE WILL BE AWARDED. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE SOLE MODIFICATION OF REDUCING THE SANCTIONS AWARD TO $1,000.00. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD.'S MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS IS GRANTED. NO LATER THAN JANUARY 31, 2019 PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND 1, LLC MUST: A) PRODUCE ALL NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO TALESUN'S THIRD SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS THAT IT HAS NOT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED TO TALESUN IN THIS CASE; B) PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG IDENTIFYING EACH DOCUMENT RESPONSIVE TO TALESUN'S THIRD SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS THAT IT HAS WITHHELD ON ANY GROUND OF PRIVILEGE; AND C) SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES STATING THAT ALL NON-PRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN PRODUCED OR ARE BEING PRODUCED CONCURRENT WITH THE FURTHER RESPONSES. MARTIFER'S FAILURE TO UNEQUIVOCALLY STATE IN VERIFIED RESPONSES THAT IT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED AND/OR WILL PRODUCE BY A DATE CERTAIN ALL NON-PRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS LACKS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND WARRANTS IMPOSITION OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. REASONABLE MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR THIS EXCEEDINGLY SIMPLE MOTION ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1000.00. MARTIFER MUST PAY THAT AMOUNT TO TALESUN NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 15, 2019. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, MARIA.TORREANOM@GMAIL.COM (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Zhongli New Energy Usa Co., Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as follows:

Defendants talesun Solar and Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. ZNE fails to maintain its burden of production.

ZNE largely relies on the Gu and Sheng declarations to assert that there is no unity of interest between ZNE and Talesun Solar USA Ltd. Their deposition testimonies show that they lack a foundation to testify regarding those issues however, and the court sustains plaintiff's objections to those declarations. Sheng declared that Talesun and ZNE have different ownership. (Sheng Dec., Paras. 4-5, 9.) At his deposition, Sheng admitted that he has no clue about who owned Talesun. (Sheng Depo., 84:2-85:6.) Gu's declaration similarly lacks foundation. At his deposition, he stated that he never heard of ZNE. (Gu Depo., 47:2-12; 68:7-9.) He further testified that he never heard of Talesun and did not work for it. (Gu Depo., 265:8-12; 37:19-24.) A month later Gu signed a declaration as Talesun's person most knowledgeable stating that there is no relationship between ZNE and Talesun. That declaration does not explain how Gu developed such an intimate knowledge of the innerworkings of the two entities in a month.

In addition, plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact as to whether alter ego liability exists. Sheng now admits that Talesun and ZNE have the same address. (Sheng Depo., 133:4-134:23.) Talesun and ZNE are both in the business of selling solar panels. (Sheng Depo., 48:14-21.) ZNE and Talesun's employees share the same e-mail addresses. (Sheng Depo., 29:9-12.) U.S. customers believed that the two entities were the same as they had the same warehouse. (Danielson Depo., 23:6-11; 30:15-18.)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD, ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd. and Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC's motion for summary adjudication on Plaintiff's first cause of action for breach of the Master Membership Interest and Partnership Agreement ("MIPA") is denied. Defendants fail to maintain their initial summary judgment burden.

Defendants do not show that they breached the MIPA on November 30, 2012 and Plaintiff filed this action in an untimely fashion more than four years later. The parties originally contemplated that the first part of the MIPA would close "on or around" November 30, 2012. The record demonstrates that the parties subsequently agreed to continue the closing date numerous times. (See Plaintiff's Ex. K [e-mail string re initial closing date]; Plaintiff's Ex. L, Chen Depo., 215:11-216:8 [Talesun employee admits that the parties agreed that the $46,160 could be paid later]; Plaintiff's Ex. M [second Recital D, 1.1(d), and exhibit B of the Standstill Agreement demonstrates that the $46,160 could be paid later]; Plaintiff's Ex. O [exhibit C to the Letter Agreement demonstrates that the $46,160 could be paid later].)

In addition, the original version of the MIPA contemplated payments on December 27, 2012 and December 31, 2012. The MIPA is fairly characterized as an installment contract and it is undisputed that a claim based on the December 27 and 31 payments is timely. Talesun cannot summarily adjudicate the entire first cause of action even if it did breach one part of the contract. (See Romano v. Rockwell Internat., Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 479, 489 [where the parties have ongoing contractual obligations, the plaintiff need not sue after an initial breach and it may elect to rely on the contract and sue at the time of complete performance]; Armstrong Petroleum Corp. v. Tri-Valley Oil & Gas Co. (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 1375, 1388 ["[W]here performance of contractual obligations is severed into intervals, as in installment contracts, the courts have found that an action attacking the performance for any particular interval must be brought within the period of limitations after the particular performance was due."]; White v. Moriarty (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1299 ["Where money is payable in installments, the statute of limitations begins to run against the cause of action for the recovery of an unpaid installment at the time it is payable."].)

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Its Second Cause Of Action Against Talesun Solar Usa Ltd
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Its Second Cause Of Action Against Talesun Solar Usa Ltd.

Plaintiff Martifer-Silverado Fund I, LLC's motion for summary adjudication on its second cause of action for breach of the Standstill Agreement is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the amount of Plaintiff's damages.

Plaintiff seeks $11,471,416.50 on this claim. That sum is derived from the Standstill Agreement and includes the base purchase price for the MIPA projects and $500,000 for Talesun's prior nonperformance. (See Standstill Agreement, Ex. B and 1.1(d).)

An injured party may recover for a breach of contract the amount which will compensate it "for all the detriment proximately caused [by the breach], or which, in the ordinary course of things, would be likely to result [from the breach]." (Civ. Code 3300.) The damages awarded should, insofar as possible, place the injured party in the same position it would have held had the contract properly been performed, but such damage may not exceed the benefit which it would have received had the promisor performed. (Civ. Code 3358.) "The right to recover damages in civil actions is qualified by the common law doctrine of avoidable consequences. Under the doctrine, 'a person injured by another's wrongful conduct will not be compensated for damages that the injured person could have avoided by reasonable effort or expenditure.'" (Mize-Kurzman v. Marin Community College Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 832, 870.)

In this case, the evidence demonstrates that Plaintiff mitigated its contractual damages by selling the MIPA projects to other entities and recovering cash and stock in return. (See Talesun's Ex. J [Howell Depo., 114:2-117:24; 120:20-121:8; 122:11-24; 123:13-125:12]; Ex. K [Isern Depo., 34:17-36:24; 41:1-42:12; 48:3-21; 90:6-91:13; 93:13-25; 116:10-16]; Ex. L [Cheney Depo., 73:1-9; 111:16-112:5; 126:2-127:4].) If Plaintiff can establish that the Standstill Agreement is enforceable and Talesun breached it, then Talesun would be entitled to an offset for these payments to prevent double recovery.

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is GRANTED. It appears amendment may foreclose time-consuming disputes over the complaint's verbiage, and "trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceedings." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) All requests for trial continuances must be directed to Department 206. Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Further Response And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ALEXA RAE HANKARD, ESQ. of Ongaro PC (Tel: 415-433-3900) appearing for plaintiff Martifer-Silverado Fund I, LLC;
Atty. JAMES GIACCHETTI, ESQ. of McManis Faulkner (Tel: 408-279-8700) appearing for defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd. and Zhongli New Energy USA Co., LLC.

Matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of reducing the sanctions award to $1,000.00. The Court rules as follows: Defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd.'s motion to compel a further response to document requests is granted. No later than January 31, 2019 plaintiff Martifer-Silverado Fund 1, LLC must: a) produce all non-privileged documents responsive to Talesun's third set of document requests that it has not previously produced to Talesun in this case; b) provide a privilege log identifying each document responsive to Talesun's third set of document requests that it has withheld on any ground of privilege; and c) serve further verified responses stating that all non-privileged responsive documents have previously been produced or are being produced concurrent with the further responses. Martifer's failure to unequivocally state in verified responses that it previously produced and/or will produce by a date certain all non-privileged responsive documents lacks substantial justification and warrants imposition of monetary sanctions. Reasonable monetary sanctions for this exceedingly simple motion are awarded in the amount of $1000.00. Martifer must pay that amount to Talesun no later than February 15, 2019. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Motion To Quash Martifer Silverado Fund I, Llcs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Reed Smith, Llp
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD's Motion To Quash Martifer Silverado Fund I, Llcs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Reed Smith, Llp.

Plaintiff served a subpoena seeking litigation-hold correspondence and related documents from defendant's former attorneys. Defendant moved to quash, citing attorney-client privilege. Plaintiff opposed the motion, arguing that defendant has failed to produce relevant emails. Plaintiff contends that the emails were in existence when defendant became aware of the possibility of litigation and should have been preserved. Plaintiff argues that federal decisions (which California often follows) permit discovery of litigation hold correspondence in cases of claimed spoliation of evidence. Plaintiff fails to cite any California authority adopting the federal doctrine of privilege. Further, while California excepts from attorney-client privilege communications to commit or plan a crime or fraud (Evidence Code Section 956), this discovery seeks the opposite: correspondence to prevent a crime or fraud. Given the absence of any California authority supporting a waiver of the attorney-client privilege here, the motion to quash will be granted. Because the case presents a good faith dispute over a novel issue of law, sanctions will be denied.

The motion to Quash is Granted. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
